description: Fix three genuine defects in the background↔side panel communication layer: duplicate notification processing from dual message paths, redundant fetchConfigState calls on sync.full, and data loss on optimistic toggle rollback with mixed tool states.

userStories.title: Eliminate duplicate notification processing in side panel ws:message fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel to process each server notification exactly once so that state updates are deterministic and not wasteful.
acceptanceCriteria: The ws:message handler in App.tsx only processes sync.full (calling loadPlugins), NOT tab.stateChanged, tool.invocationStart, or tool.invocationEnd
Notification methods that the background forwards via sp:serverMessage (tab.stateChanged, tool.invocationStart, tool.invocationEnd, plugins.changed) are NOT handled in the ws:message branch
The sp:serverMessage handler continues to handle all notification types as before
handleNotification is only called from the sp:serverMessage branch, not the ws:message branch

userStories.title: Deduplicate fetchConfigState calls on sync.full
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to make only one config.getState request after sync.full so that the MCP server is not hit with redundant requests.
acceptanceCriteria: After a sync.full, exactly one config.getState request is sent (not two)
plugins.changed from handlePluginUpdate still triggers a config.getState fetch (this path must remain working)
plugins.changed from handleSyncFull does NOT trigger a redundant fetch if sync.full already triggered one within the debounce window
A simple debounce (100ms) or dedup guard prevents double-fetching without dropping legitimate plugins.changed notifications from plugin.update

userStories.title: Remove unnecessary loadPluginsRef indirection in App.tsx
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the App component to use loadPlugins directly instead of through a ref, reducing indirection since loadPlugins already has a stable identity from useCallback with empty deps.
acceptanceCriteria: All call sites that used loadPluginsRef.current() now call loadPlugins() directly
loadPlugins is added to the useEffect dependency array (it has stable identity from useCallback([]))
The behavior is identical: loadPlugins is called on connect and on relevant notifications

userStories.title: Fix PluginCard optimistic toggle-all rollback to preserve per-tool states
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'toggle all' switch rollback on server error to restore the exact per-tool enabled states that existed before I clicked, not a uniform opposite value.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToggleAll captures the per-tool enabled states before applying the optimistic update
On server error, the rollback restores the exact captured per-tool states
If tools had mixed state (some enabled, some disabled) before toggle-all, the rollback restores that exact mixed state
The optimistic update behavior (immediate UI response) is preserved
